name: Obsidian
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Obsidian's brand language — the personal-knowledge-management tool that worships the knowledge graph. Canvas is a deep slate-purple-black (#202020) in dark mode (the default), or a parchment-cream (#f6f3ee) in light. The signature visual element is the force-directed graph of nodes — purple glowing dots connected by faint white edges — that appears as both the product's centerpiece and the marketing hero. Type is a precise grotesk (Inter / Söhne) with markdown-derived hierarchy: H1 / H2 / H3 styled to mirror raw markdown rendering. The brand reads as 'private, infinite, sovereign' — the antithesis of cloud-collab.

design_language.motion_signature: Knowledge-graph nodes drift in slow physics simulation — gentle force-directed jiggle, ~0.2 Hz. Edges glow faintly purple on hover. New-node animation pulses out from center over 600ms. Markdown preview updates use a 120ms cross-fade. The product chrome is otherwise still — sovereignty is calmness.

trademark_signals: Force-directed knowledge graph — purple glowing nodes connected by faint white edges on a slate-purple-black canvas — appears as the marketing hero, the product centerpiece, and as decorative motif inside feature blocks
Dark slate-purple canvas (#202020) as default — Obsidian is a 'dark-mode-first' brand; light mode is the second-class citizen
Markdown-rendered hierarchy on the marketing page itself — H1s with the same styling as in-product H1, code-blocks in identical syntax-highlight palette as the product
Plugin-grid ecosystem showcase — 1500+ community plugins displayed as a dense card grid is itself a marketing device, reinforcing the 'infinitely extensible' message
Long-form feature explanations with screenshot-as-illustration — Obsidian shows actual product screenshots (not abstract gradient art) embedded in editorial-length paragraphs
ai_slop_avoided: Don't paint the canvas pure black. The slight purple-tint slate (#202020) is the brand differentiator from generic dark-mode SaaS
Don't render the knowledge graph as a flat illustration. It must look like the real product output — physics-simulated, glowing, connected — never a static SVG diagram
Don't add saturated accent colors. The brand is monochrome-on-slate with a single purple voltage; introducing teal/orange/red breaks the sovereignty-and-focus voice

Don't show cloud-sync as a primary value proposition. Obsidian's brand is 'your notes, on your machine, forever'; messaging around cloud or collaboration reads as off-brand (Obsidian Sync is a paid add-on, not the lead)
Don't use stock illustrations. Every visual on an Obsidian marketing page is either a product screenshot, a knowledge-graph render, or a plugin tile
